Read the following passage and answer the question: What is special about the boy's face?: FRECKLES In a corner of the park, many children were lining up waiting for a painter to decorate their faces to become "Indians" or "aliens",.... A boy also held his grandmother's hand and waited for his turn. The boy's face had many small freckles, but his eyes lit up with excitement. - You have so many freckles, where is there any place on your face to draw! - The girl in line behind the boy said loudly. Embarrassed, the boy lowered his head. Seeing that, his grandmother also sat down next to him: "Why are you so sad? I love your freckles! When I was little, I always wanted freckles." Then the old woman stroked the boy's cheek with her wrinkled fingers: Freckles are also very pretty, I'm sure the painter will like your freckles!". The boy smiled. - Really, grandma? Really! - His grandmother replied.. Here, try to find something more beautiful than freckles. The boy looked at her, thought for a moment, then whispered: - Wrinkles, grandmother!
B: The boy's face has a lot of pimples.
C: There is a big scar on the face

Question: Read the following passage and answer the question: What season is summer in the author's mind?: THE SOUND OF THE KITE_x000D_ _x000D_ I don't know since when, but summer has been imprinted in my mind. It is the season of kites filled with wind, the season of souls with childhood aspirations._x000D_ _x000D_ Every afternoon, when the bright sunlight gradually fades, it is also the time when we children rush out to the fields, tossing small kites into the sky. Putting aside our worries about schoolwork, we long to watch the kites in the vast sky. Flying kites in the windy afternoon, listening to the whistling sound of the flute mixed with the cheers of the children. No piece of music by a genius artist can compare to that piece of music of the countryside. The sound of the flute is clear and pure like the call of summer, the call of souls searching for childhood memories._x000D_ _x000D_ I have been away from the kite wings of my childhood for quite a long time. The sound of the kite flute still lingers in my dreams, evoking a memory._x000D_ _x000D_ Another summer has come. I put on my backpack and return to my hometown with the urging sound of the kite flute. I caught the image of young boys engrossed in bamboo strips making kites like I was in the past. Suddenly, the sound of the kite flute rose up and hummed over the peaceful fields, making me stunned. I realized so many things in that flute sound... Oh. the kite flute... perhaps it will follow me throughout this life...
A: It is the season of delicious, fragrant fruits.
B: It's the season of lullabies every summer afternoon.
C: It's the season of memories of playing with friends on the windy fields.
D: It is the season of kites filled with wind, of souls yearning for childhood.

Question: Read the following text silently: THE STORY OF MOTHER GOOSE One day, the mother goose took her golden goslings for a walk. The goslings began to spread out across the meadow. At that moment, large hailstones fell from the sky. The goslings ran to their mother. The mother goose spread her wings to cover her goslings. Under their wings, the goslings felt warm and comfortable. Then everything became quiet again. The geese immediately insisted: "Let us go, mother!" The mother goose gently lifted her wings and the goslings ran out onto the lawn. They saw their mother's wet wings and tattered feathers. But the light, the meadow, the orange wings, the honeybees made them forget to ask their mother what was wrong. Only one goose, the smallest and weakest, ran to her mother and asked: "Why are your wings so torn?" The mother goose softly replied: "Everything is fine, my child!" The goslings scattered on the grass again and the mother goose felt happy. (VASKHOMMILLISKI) * Based on the content of the reading, circle the letter before the most correct answer or do as required in the exercise below. Read the following text silently: THE STORY OF MOTHER GOOSE One day, the mother goose took her golden goslings for a walk. The goslings began to scatter across the grass. At that moment, large hailstones fell from the sky. The goslings ran to their mother. The mother goose spread her wings to cover her goslings. Under their wings, the goslings felt warm and comfortable. Then everything became quiet again. The geese immediately insisted: "Let us go, mother!" The mother goose gently lifted her wings and the goslings ran out onto the grass. They saw their mother's wet wings and tattered feathers. But the light, the grass, the orange wings, the honey bees made them forget to ask their mother what was wrong. Question: Silently read the following text: DA VILLAGE SCENE Winter has truly arrived! Clouds from above slide down the mountain slopes, occasionally scattering a light rain on the silver-white thatched roofs. The yellow mustard flowers appear in long strips in the mist on the hillside. The noisy, winding stream has shrunk, revealing smooth and clean pebbles. On the water surface, only the long-legged spiders like busy and happy pincers compete to go upstream. On the tops of the old, ancient trees, the last remaining yellow leaves are rustling before saying goodbye to their simple mother. But the rows of Da village areca trees, despite all the brutal power of winter, still have their leaves hanging gently like swallows' tails. On the ground hardened by the cold, the young leaves are still spreading, pale yellow and the areca trees are still graceful, swaying their bodies, as if they were born to decorate Da village with a more elegant and gentle look.
